title: lithocarpus hancei
intro: Lithocarpus hancei, also known as the Hance's Lithocarpus or Hance's evergreen oak, is a species of evergreen tree in the beech family, native to southern China. The bark and leaves of Lithocarpus hancei have been used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids, triterpenoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antioxidant, antiviral, and anticancer properties, providing potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. Lithocarpus hancei can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, as well as being used in traditional Chinese medicine as a powder or capsule.
